title: Travel agent — app:form widget for refining Amadeus filters before re-running
description: The travel agent's render_* widget trees today are read-only — once the user sees a flight/hotel/location/activity result (or a friendly Amadeus error), the only way to refine the search is to type a new prompt. This round adds an app:form refinement widget appended to each render_* tree. The form's fields are pre-populated with the current intent's parameters; the submit button emits a `key: "command"` widget event with a templated natural-language travel query that gets substituted at click time from the current field values. Both terminal (NoetlPrompt) and canvas (GatewayAssistant) already handle command-style widget events. The ONLY missing primitive is template substitution in AppForm.tsx — the current click handler emits either the static button.event.value or the form values dict, neither of which works for 'build a command from current values'. This round adds that, then plumbs the form into each render_* tree. Cap: 1 gui PR + 1 ops PR + 1 docs PR. Sequencing: gui PR must merge AND the cluster must redeploy gui before the ops PR opens.

design_brief_for_codex.current_state_gui: repos/gui/src/components/widgets/AppForm.tsx click handler is `value: button.event?.value ?? values`. Either static string OR form values dict. No template substitution. Both NoetlPrompt.handleWidgetEvent and GatewayAssistant's inline onWidgetEvent handle `key: 'command'` with a string value (terminal calls runCommand(value); canvas calls commandToCanvasPrompt(value, message) + onSubmit). So a string command works in both surfaces; a dict does not.
design_brief_for_codex.gui_test_coverage: Add a unit test (Jest/Testing Library, mirroring existing widget tests in repos/gui/src/components/widgets/__tests__/) covering: (a) static string value emitted as-is when no placeholders; (b) {fieldId} placeholders substituted from values dict; (c) missing field becomes empty string; (d) non-string value (undefined, array, object) preserves existing fall-back behaviour. Aim for the smallest test that exercises all four shapes.
design_brief_for_codex.no_classifier_changes: Do NOT modify classify_intent or its system prompt — the form just reuses the current classification's fields as form defaults. The classifier already extracts everything we need.
design_brief_for_codex.tutorial_07_update: repos/docs/docs/tutorials/07-travel-agent-with-widgets.md — add a new subsection in Step 4 (Widget output) titled 'Refinement forms' that shows the app:form template-substitution pattern. Cite the AppForm.tsx click handler and explain the {fieldId} placeholder convention. One short example per intent (the form widget JSON) is enough.

1_gui_appform_template_substitution.objective: Land template substitution in AppForm.tsx with test coverage.
spec: `cd repos/gui && git checkout -b kadyapam/appform-template-substitution`.
Edit src/components/widgets/AppForm.tsx click handler per the design brief.
Add a unit test covering the four behaviours.
Verify: `npm test -- AppForm` (or whatever the project's test invocation is) passes locally.
Verify: `npm run build` clean.

Commit: `feat(widgets): app:form button template substitution with {fieldId} placeholders`. Body explains: travel agent refinement forms need string command emission with form values substituted at click time; existing widget event plumbing in NoetlPrompt and GatewayAssistant already handles string command events; this is the missing primitive.
PR title: same. Push, PR, merge with `--squash --delete-branch`.
1_gui_appform_template_substitution.verify: GUI PR merged. Semantic-release auto-bumps a minor version (feat: triggers minor). Cluster redeploy of gui happens via the standard BumpAndDeploy pipeline.

2_redeploy_gui.objective: Confirm the new gui release is live in the GKE cluster before opening the ops PR.
spec: Wait for the semantic-release auto-tag (likely v1.x.x with minor bump).
Confirm the new tag is built and pushed to ghcr.io.
Bump the gui gitlink in ai-meta locally (will be folded into the final pointer-bump commit).
Confirm the GKE deployment is rolled to the new image: `kubectl -n noetl get deployment noetl-gui -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[*].image}'`.
2_redeploy_gui.verify: GKE noetl-gui image tag matches the new release.

3_ops_render_widget_forms.objective: Append app:form refinement widget to each render_* step in the travel runtime.
spec: `cd repos/ops && git checkout -b kadyapam/travel-render-refinement-forms`.
Edit automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml: add the app:form widget to the children list of each render_*'s app:column. Use the per-intent shapes in the design brief.
For render_amadeus_failure: introspect classify_intent.intent at render time and emit the matching form field set + command template.
Pydantic-validate via `repos/noetl/.venv/bin/python -c '...'`.
Commit: `feat(travel-agent): refinement form appended to each render_* widget tree`. Body cites the gui#XX template-substitution PR and explains the per-intent form shapes.
PR title: same. Push, PR, merge.
3_ops_render_widget_forms.verify: Ops PR merged. Travel runtime registers cleanly in the catalog.

spec: `cd repos/docs && git checkout -b kadyapam/travel-tutorial-refinement-forms`.

Edit docs/tutorials/07-travel-agent-with-widgets.md — new subsection 'Refinement forms' under Step 4 (Widget output). Show the app:form template-substitution pattern with one short example. Cite the AppForm.tsx {fieldId} convention.
Verify: `npm install && npm run build` clean.
Commit: `docs(tutorials): document travel agent refinement-form widgets`.
Push, PR, merge.

5_re_register_and_smoke.objective: Re-register travel runtime; smoke all four intents; click-test the refinement form on each.
spec: Pull merged ops at HEAD; `noetl register` the patched runtime; capture catalog version.
Smoke `travel flights ...` — expect render_flights tree includes app:form. Click 'Refine search' with edited values; expect a new execution with the substituted command.
Smoke `travel hotels in Paris ...` — same; expect refinement form, click-test, new execution.
Smoke `travel locations near Boston` — same.
Smoke `travel activities near Times Square` — same.
Smoke at least one Amadeus-failure scenario (the test API has been 500ing) — expect render_amadeus_failure tree includes form keyed to the classified intent.
Capture screenshots of the form rendered inline + the resulting new execution.
5_re_register_and_smoke.verify: Form rendered + clickable in each intent. New execution started on click. Substituted command visible in the resulting prompt entry.

6_bump_ai_meta_pointers.objective: Bump repos/gui + repos/ops + repos/docs gitlinks in ai-meta. Stage but do not push.
spec: `cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/gui repos/ops repos/docs`.
`git add repos/gui repos/ops repos/docs`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump gui + ops + docs for travel agent refinement-form widgets`.
Don't push ai-meta — Kadyapam pushes.
6_bump_ai_meta_pointers.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit covering all three submodule bumps.

expected_state.description: Each travel render_* widget tree includes an app:form refinement widget that emits a templated travel command on submit. Both surfaces (terminal + canvas) re-run with the substituted query.
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; HAS_FORM=$(grep -c 'app:form' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); HAS_TEMPLATE_SUB=$(grep -c 'replace.*\\{.*\\w' repos/gui/src/components/widgets/AppForm.tsx 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "app_form_uses:$HAS_FORM template_sub:$HAS_TEMPLATE_SUB"; if [ "$HAS_FORM" -ge 4 ] && [ "$HAS_TEMPLATE_SUB" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: REFINEMENT_FORMS_LANDED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Modifying repos/noetl, repos/e2e — gui + ops + docs only round
Modifying classify_intent or its system prompt
Modifying the Amadeus or Vertex AI MCP playbooks
Re-introducing direct urllib calls to Amadeus from the travel runtime
Opening the ops PR before the gui PR is merged AND deployed
preferred_tools: cd repos/gui && npm test / npm run build
cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build
git apply / git commit -F - / git push
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
Browser screenshot for the form click-tests
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260510-201500-travel-app-form-refinement.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_gui_appform_template_substitution: {pr_url, merged_sha, gui_release_tag, tests_passing}, 2_redeploy_gui: {gke_image_tag}, 3_ops_render_widget_forms: {pr_url, merged_sha, validates}, 4_open_and_merge_docs_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha, docs_build_clean}, 5_re_register_and_smoke: {travel_catalog_id, travel_version, flights_form_visible: bool, hotels_form_visible: bool, locations_form_visible: bool, activities_form_visible: bool, failure_form_visible: bool, refinement_click_starts_new_execution: bool, screenshots: []}, 6_bump_ai_meta_pointers: {gui_sha, ops_sha, docs_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups}. If verdict GREEN, append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md.
